title: Micromeles subochracea
taxonomy: Micromeles subochracea is a species of plant in the family Rosaceae. It is classified as a type of shrub or small tree. Micromeles subochracea is native to the mountainous regions of Asia, specifically in China, India, Nepal, and Bhutan. This plant belongs to the genus Micromeles, which is closely related to the genus Cotoneaster. Further classification of Micromeles subochracea places it within the order Rosales.

Micromeles subochracea, commonly known as Chinese hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China. Chinese hawthorn is used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Chinese hawthorn include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chinese hawthorn include tea, tablets, and capsules, as well as fruit that can be consumed fresh or dried.
